title: Service Profile Association Failures (Cisco UCS)
spl:
index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults")
| search assoc_state="failed" OR match(lower(descr),"(?i)association.*fail")
| stats count by sp_name, server_dn, descr
| sort -count
description: Failed or stuck service profile associations block server bring-up and maintenance — complements compliance state (UC-19.1.2).
value: Failed or stuck service profile associations block server bring-up and maintenance — complements compliance state (UC-19.1.2).
grandmaExplanation: Failed or stuck service profile associations block server bring-up and maintenance — complements compliance state (UC-19.1.2) — so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
implementation: Tune search to UCS fault codes for association. Alert on any failed association in production orgs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ucs:config`, UCS faults.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Tune search to UCS fault codes for association. Alert on any failed association in production orgs.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_ucs (sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config" OR sourcetype="cisco:ucs:faults")
| search assoc_state="failed" OR match(lower(descr),"(?i)association.*fail")
| stats count by sp_name, server_dn, descr
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Service Profile Association Failures (Cisco UCS)** — Failed or stuck service profile associations block server bring-up and maintenance — complements compliance state (UC-19.1.2).

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ucs:config`, UCS faults. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_ucs; **sourcetype**: cisco:ucs:config, cisco:ucs:faults. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_ucs, sourcetype="cisco:ucs:config". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sp_name, server_dn, descr** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (failed associations), Timeline, Single value (open failures).
visualization: Table (failed associations), Timeline, Single value (open failures).
